_note: Seed prompts are just examples. The pipeline accepts ANY task — domain is only a metadata label.

naming_branding.label: Naming & Branding
prompts: Generate names for an AI-powered creative studio that helps musicians compose
Create a brand name for a biotech startup focused on gut microbiome therapeutics
Name a nonprofit that teaches critical thinking to teenagers through debate
Design a tagline for an AI ethics consultancy that audits algorithms for bias
Rebrand a 100-year-old public library system for the digital age
Name a climate-tech company that converts atmospheric CO2 into building materials
Create a name for an app that pairs lonely elderly people with student volunteers
Name a speculative design studio that prototypes solutions for problems 20 years from now
Generate names for a multilingual education platform serving refugee communities
Name a wearable device that monitors stress through skin conductance and suggests interventions

cross_domain_analogy.label: Cross-Domain Analogy
prompts: Find a deep structural analogy between immune systems and market regulation
Map jazz improvisation onto software architecture design patterns
Draw a structural analogy between coral reef ecosystems and open-source communities
Find parallels between how forests recover from fire and how teams recover from failure
Map the structure of a symphony orchestra onto distributed computing systems
Find a deep analogy between how rivers form deltas and how startups find product-market fit
Draw structural parallels between the evolution of language and the evolution of programming languages
Map fermentation processes onto how ideas mature in creative teams
Find an analogy between how mycelial networks share nutrients and how knowledge spreads in organizations
Draw parallels between plate tectonics and paradigm shifts in science

scientific_hypothesis.label: Scientific Hypothesis
prompts: Generate hypotheses for why a promising Alzheimer's drug trial failed despite strong preclinical results
Propose alternative mechanisms for the observed placebo effect in pain management
Generate hypotheses for why identical twins sometimes develop different autoimmune diseases
Propose novel explanations for the Fermi paradox that haven't been widely discussed
Generate hypotheses for why sleep deprivation impairs creativity more than analytical reasoning

strategic_reframing.label: Strategic Problem Reframing
prompts: Reframe 'how do we increase school attendance?' as a systems problem with at least 3 alternative definitions
Reframe 'how do we reduce hospital readmissions?' by questioning what 'success' actually means
Find three alternative definitions of 'productivity' that would change what a company optimizes for
Reframe 'how do we fight misinformation?' by questioning the assumption that more information is the solution
Reframe 'how do we make AI safer?' by exploring what 'safety' means to different stakeholders

speculative_design.label: Speculative & Future Design
prompts: Design a society that has solved loneliness as a public health problem
What does education look like when AI tutors are ubiquitous and free?
Design a healthcare system optimized for prevention rather than treatment
What does democratic governance look like when real-time citizen input is technologically trivial?
Design a city where no commute exceeds 15 minutes and explain the second-order effects

narrative_character.label: Narrative & Character Design
prompts: Create a villain whose worldview is internally coherent and sympathetic
Design a non-human character with genuinely alien cognition that isn't just 'smart but weird'
Build a protagonist defined by a contradiction they never resolve
Design a story world where a physical law works differently and explore the social consequences
Create a mentor character whose advice is genuinely wrong but given in good faith

philosophical_thought.label: Philosophical Thought Experiments
prompts: Construct a thought experiment challenging the hard problem of consciousness
Reframe the trolley problem using a non-Western ethical framework
Design a thought experiment that reveals a hidden assumption in how we define intelligence
Construct an argument that makes a widely held moral intuition seem clearly wrong
Propose a new philosophical paradox about identity that hasn't been named yet

product_ideation.label: Product & System Ideation
prompts: Design an offline literacy tool for rural classrooms with no reliable electricity
Propose a novel approach to vaccine distribution tracking that works without smartphones
Design a mental health support system for contexts where therapy is culturally stigmatized
Invent a tool that helps first-generation university students navigate academic bureaucracy
Design a peer learning system that works in classrooms with 60+ students and one teacher

everyday_creative.label: Everyday Creative Tasks
prompts: Find a name for my sausage dog
Design a dinner party theme for 8 people who have nothing in common
Write a toast for a wedding where you barely know the couple
Come up with a birthday present idea for someone who says they don't want anything
Design a morning routine for someone who hates mornings

eval_held_out: What project ideas would you generate for the Gemma 4 Good Hackathon that would genuinely impress judges looking for novelty, technical depth, and real-world impact?
Design a creative mentorship system for students in under-resourced schools that doesn't require internet access
Generate names for a new field of study that combines neuroscience, design thinking, and indigenous knowledge systems
